basic_details.job_title: Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri Recruitment 2026 - 333 Vacancies, Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: Office of the District Programme Officer (Child Development), Shahjahanpur
basic_details.short_information: The Office of the District Programme Officer (Child Development), Shahjahanpur has invited online applications for 333 Anganwadi Karyakartri (Anganwadi Worker) posts across 16 Child Development Projects in the district's rural and urban areas. This is a direct recruitment for honorarium-based vacant posts. Applications opened on 13 July 2026 and will close on 3 August 2026 at 11:59 PM. Only local women candidates are eligible, and applications must be submitted online through the vacancy management portal.

age_limit.details: Minimum age: 18 years Maximum age: 35 years Age will be calculated as on the cut-off date: 1 July 2026. Date of birth recorded in the High School marksheet/certificate will be considered valid for age calculation.

qualification.details: Only local women candidates are eligible to apply. Minimum educational qualification: Intermediate (12th) or equivalent. The candidate must be a resident of the ward (in case of an urban local body) or the gram panchayat (in case of a rural area) where the vacant post is located. The entire selection process will be governed by Government Order No. 1/1092245/2025/3313/58-1-2025 (1917687) dated 17 September 2025.

salary_details.details: The notification confirms these are honorarium-based (मानदेय आधारित) posts, but the exact honorarium amount is not specified in the notification.

selection_process: Selection is merit-based. Candidates must hold a minimum qualification of Intermediate or equivalent. Merit is determined by adding the marks of Graduate or Postgraduate qualifications (where applicable) to the Intermediate or equivalent marks
calculated through the departmental portal as per the method laid out in Clause 7
A post reserved for a specific category will accept applications only from candidates of that category. Unreserved category posts are open to candidates of any caste.
Widow candidates: A death certificate issued by the Gram Panchayat Officer/concerned Urban Local Body
or a certified copy of the family register

will be accepted. Divorced/deserted candidates: A legal order issued by a competent court will be accepted. Income certificate: Only income certificates issued per UP Government Order No. 3959/1-9-11-Raj-9 dated 30.09.2011 (as amended) by a competent authority

will be accepted. Validity of the income certificate is calculated from the date of publication of this notification. Residence and caste certificate: Only digitally signed residence and caste certificates issued online by the Tehsil
will be accepted. Candidates must declare widow/divorced/deserted status at the time of filling the online application form itself. Claims made after submission
without this declaration in the form
how_to_apply: Visit the vacancy management portal: http://upanganwadibharti.in
Fill the online application form carefully and accurately in the format available on the portal.
Upload a clean, legible, self-attested copy of all documents referenced in the online form.
Declare widow/divorced/deserted status at the time of application, if applicable — this cannot be claimed later.
Submit the application within the prescribed time window. Offline application forms will not be accepted under any circumstances.
Applications must be submitted by 3 August 2026, 11:59 PM.
important_instructions: Only local women candidates are eligible for the advertised posts. Candidates are solely responsible for the accuracy of all facts, figures, and educational marks/percentages entered in the application. Any error or falsification found may result in rejection of candidature. In case of any spelling or data-entry discrepancy on the online portal, the details recorded in the original documents will be treated as final and correct. The number of vacancies may increase or decrease; the updated number will be shown on the official website. Detailed eligibility criteria and the application process are also available on the departmental website, http://upanganwadibharti.in. Further information can be obtained from the Tehsil Office, Block Development Office, Child Development Project Officer Office, or the District Programme Officer (Child Development) Office at Vikas Bhawan, Shahjahanpur, during office hours.

rows.details: Office of the District Programme Officer (Child Development), Shahjahanpur

content_sections.heading: Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri Project-wise Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 333 vacancies have been notified across 16 Child Development Projects (Bal Vikas Pariyojna) in Shahjahanpur district. The number of vacancies may increase or decrease, and the updated figures will be shown on the official website. Full village-wise and ward-wise reservation and eligibility details have been displayed on the notice boards of the District Collectorate, Vikas Bhawan, Tehsil Vikas Khand, and Child Development Project offices, and have also been uploaded to the vacancy management portal.

content_sections.heading: Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Only local women candidates are eligible to apply. Minimum educational qualification: Intermediate (12th) or equivalent. The candidate must be a resident of the ward (in case of an urban local body) or the gram panchayat (in case of a rural area) where the vacant post is located. The entire selection process will be governed by Government Order No. 1/1092245/2025/3313/58-1-2025 (1917687) dated 17 September 2025.

content_sections.heading: Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri Age Limit
content_sections.text: Minimum age: 18 years Maximum age: 35 years Age will be calculated as on the cut-off date: 1 July 2026. Date of birth recorded in the High School marksheet/certificate will be considered valid for age calculation.

content_sections.heading: Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri Salary/Honorarium
content_sections.text: The notification confirms these are honorarium-based (मानदेय आधारित) posts, but the exact honorarium amount is not specified in the notification.

content_sections.heading: Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is merit-based. Candidates must hold a minimum qualification of Intermediate or equivalent. Merit is determined by adding the marks of Graduate or Postgraduate qualifications (where applicable) to the Intermediate or equivalent marks, calculated through the departmental portal as per the method laid out in Clause 7, points (I) to (IV), of the governing Government Order. A post reserved for a specific category will accept applications only from candidates of that category. Unreserved category posts are open to candidates of any caste. Widow candidates: A death certificate issued by the Gram Panchayat Officer/concerned Urban Local Body, or a certified copy of the family register, will be accepted. Divorced/deserted candidates: A legal order issued by a competent court will be accepted. Income certificate: Only income certificates issued per UP Government Order No. 3959/1-9-11-Raj-9 dated 30.09.2011 (as amended) by a competent authority, verifiable online, will be accepted. Validity of the income certificate is calculated from the date of publication of this notification. Residence and caste certificate: Only digitally signed residence and caste certificates issued online by the Tehsil, verifiable online, will be accepted. Candidates must declare widow/divorced/deserted status at the time of filling the online application form itself. Claims made after submission, without this declaration in the form, will not be accepted.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the vacancy management portal: http://upanganwadibharti.in Fill the online application form carefully and accurately in the format available on the portal. Upload a clean, legible, self-attested copy of all documents referenced in the online form. Declare widow/divorced/deserted status at the time of application, if applicable — this cannot be claimed later. Submit the application within the prescribed time window. Offline application forms will not be accepted under any circumstances. Applications must be submitted by 3 August 2026, 11:59 PM.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Only local women candidates are eligible for the advertised posts. Candidates are solely responsible for the accuracy of all facts, figures, and educational marks/percentages entered in the application. Any error or falsification found may result in rejection of candidature. In case of any spelling or data-entry discrepancy on the online portal, the details recorded in the original documents will be treated as final and correct. The number of vacancies may increase or decrease; the updated number will be shown on the official website. Detailed eligibility criteria and the application process are also available on the departmental website, http://upanganwadibharti.in. Further information can be obtained from the Tehsil Office, Block Development Office, Child Development Project Officer Office, or the District Programme Officer (Child Development) Office at Vikas Bhawan, Shahjahanpur, during office hours.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online is 3 August 2026, till 11:59 PM.

faq.question: Q2. How many Anganwadi Karyakartri vacancies are there in Shahjahanpur?
faq.answer: 333 vacancies have been notified across 16 Child Development Projects in Shahjahanpur district.

faq.question: Q3. Who is eligible to apply for Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri posts?
faq.answer: Only local women candidates who are residents of the ward (urban) or gram panchayat (rural) of the vacant post, and who hold a minimum qualification of Intermediate or equivalent, are eligible.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be a minimum of 18 years and a maximum of 35 years of age, as on the cut-off date of 1 July 2026.

faq.question: Q5. Where can I apply for Shahjahanpur Anganwadi Karyakartri Recruitment?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online only, through the vacancy management portal at upanganwadibharti.in. Offline applications are not accepted.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary for Anganwadi Karyakartri in Shahjahanpur?
faq.answer: The notification confirms this is an honorarium-based post but does not specify the exact honorarium amount.
